summary: A native desktop application for automated drone footage analysis and highlight reel generation. Built with Rust, Tauri, React, TypeScript, OpenCV, CLIP embeddings, Claude API, and FFmpeg.
description: SkyClip is a desktop AI video editor that takes raw drone footage and automatically identifies the best clips, analyzes scenes semantically, and compiles polished highlight reels. Built as a native app with Tauri (Rust backend) for fast local processing without cloud dependencies.

key_features.scene_analysis: CLIP embeddings for semantic scene understanding — identifies what's in each frame (ocean, sunset, buildings, action) without manual tagging
key_features.intelligent_selection: Claude API for intelligent clip selection and sequencing based on visual content and storytelling flow
key_features.video_processing: OpenCV handles frame extraction and video analysis; FFmpeg powers the final highlight reel compilation
key_features.native_desktop: Built with Tauri (Rust) for fast, native performance — processes video locally without uploading to the cloud

why_this_matters: SkyClip demonstrates Matt's range beyond web development — native desktop apps, Rust, computer vision, and AI-driven video processing. It combines multiple AI technologies (CLIP, Claude) with systems-level programming (Rust, FFmpeg) in a practical creative tool.
